title: Feastables - Interactive Trivia Game Email
description: Trivia-themed interactive email built with Spellbound.io that garnered engagement from thousands of subscribers. Triggered different flow-specific screens based on user clicks, encouraging email re-opens. A masterclass in gamified email engagement.
conversion_data: Engagement from thousands of subscribers; encouraged email re-opens

title: Feastables - Don't Click Reverse Psychology Email
description: Reverse psychology CTA telling readers NOT to click. Multi-screen interactive journey with cheeky, silly original copy that drove massive engagement. The 'forbidden fruit' technique applied to email marketing.

title: True Botanicals - Liquid Gold Hero Banner
description: Bold hero banner with golden-hued liquid design that mimics the texture of skincare oils. The design visually reinforces nourishment and glow. Achieved 20% higher click rate versus flat rectangular banner.
conversion_data: 20% higher click rate vs flat rectangular banner

title: Fenty Beauty - Minimalist Header Product Announcement
description: Minimalist header with just eyebrow copy and logo. Simple, streamlined approach that lets the product photography do the heavy lifting. Strategic use of white space creates premium feel.

title: AURA BORA - Image-Led Storytelling Without Product
description: Pushing storytelling through high-quality images with minimal text. Humorous tone with no product showcase at all. Exemplifies the minimalist trend of letting brand personality shine through imagery alone.

title: Jeni's - Halloween Seasonal Campaign with Cheeky Visuals
description: Balanced vibrant imagery with substantial copy. Purple background with orange CTA button creates Halloween mood. Cheeky, fun images paired with explanatory paragraph. Kid-sized Halloween gift cards available in scoop shops only.

title: Who Gives A Crap - Welcome Email with Land Acknowledgement Footer
description: Creative footer design with playful brand icons and sincere land acknowledgement. Spaced social links. The footer alone builds brand respect and differentiates through values-driven design.

title: Buoy - Product Comparison Email with Visual Hierarchy
description: Clean, modern design with checkmarks/Xs comparing product advantages against competitors. Prominent CTA against dark purple background. Easily digestible comparison format that drives purchase decisions.

title: Wool and the Gang - iMessage-Style Conversational Email
description: Uses conversational tone mimicking friend-to-friend iMessage exchanges with GIFs. Creates a familiar, relatable communication style that evokes liking and persuasion. Speaks the buyer's language in a format they already use daily.

title: Function of Beauty - Handwritten Apology Letter Email
description: Creative handwritten apology letter design addressed to the subscriber's hair. Addresses pain points through unique visual storytelling. The handwritten aesthetic breaks every email design convention to create something memorable.

title: Brooklinen - Mystery Sale Unwrapping GIF
description: Creates curiosity with mystery unwrapping GIF animation. Teases discount without revealing amount. Integrates with calendar apps for sale reminders. The GIF-driven mystery builds anticipation that drives opens and clicks.

subject_line: Lemme magically transport you back to your cart. Free of charge.
title: Chubbies - Black Friday Blurred Product Teaser
description: Uses absurd, humorous sender name and subject line. Teases new products with blurred images creating mystery. Promotes branded 'Thighber Monday' event. The humor and blurred imagery are uniquely Chubbies.

title: Tuft & Needle - 3-Part Objection-Handling Abandoned Cart Series
description: Three-email abandoned cart flow addressing different objections in each: Email 1 acknowledges mattress shopping pain points, Email 2 provides transparent competitor comparison, Email 3 emphasizes satisfaction guarantee. Each email links to dedicated landing pages for objection handling.

title: Beardbrand - Personalized Beard Growth Re-engagement
description: Instead of guilt-tripping lapsed subscribers, uses personalized beard growth fact ('Your beard grew 1.5 inches since we last saw you'). Sent from founder. Includes products with focus on health benefits. Clever data-driven personalization that delights.

title: Glossier - Subscriber Appreciation with Hidden Product Links
description: Heartfelt thank you email without any visible promotional elements. Every clickable element secretly links to products while maintaining pure appreciation tone. Compliments subscribers for engaging with the brand. Subtle conversion design disguised as gratitude.

title: Apple - Minimalist Product Launch with Dual CTAs
description: Quintessential minimalist product launch design keeping the product image centered. High-quality product photography with massive white space. Dual CTAs (buy/learn more) give subscribers choice without clutter. The gold standard of product launch emails.

title: Apple - VisionPro Pre-Order with FOMO Appeal
description: Visual elegance and economy for VisionPro VR goggles pre-order. High-impact product image dominates with sparse supporting text. The deliberately minimal approach creates premium desire and urgency simultaneously.

title: Dior - Jewel-Toned Luxury Fashion Email
description: Jewel shades with a single green jungle leaf to accentuate vivid shoes. Oozing luxury and high-end elegance. Every element deliberately curated to convey aspirational brand positioning through color and composition.

title: American Apparel - Rainbow Product Grid Email
description: Rainbow of products arranged in a visually enticing, irreverent, and unexpected layout. Pure eyeball candy that converts. Breaks conventional grid layouts to create something visually magnetic.

title: Absolut - Wall-to-Wall Electric Blue Brand Email
description: Wall-to-wall electric blue combined with big, bold, punchy font. Pure brand consistency with signature colors. No compromise on brand identity. The email IS the brand.

title: Nike - Deliberately Sparse Site Launch Teaser
description: Deliberately sparse layout to pique curiosity for a site launch. Minimal information builds intrigue. Proves that what you leave out of an email can be more powerful than what you put in.

title: Headspace - Positive Last-Chance Trial Expiration
description: Puts a positive, empowering spin on urgency tactics instead of guilt-tripping. Simple, colorful images create cheerful tone even for a trial expiration notice. Proves urgency emails don't have to be stressful.

title: Collaborative Fund - Red & Yellow Textured Newsletter
description: Limited color palette of red and yellow with crumpled paper textures. Bold color bursts with clean horizontal divides. Incorporating textures via high-quality graphics makes the 2D email experience more visceral and engaging.

title: Litmus - Email Design Conference with Live Twitter Feed
description: Bold color burst header with whimsical illustrations and extensive white space. Integrated live Twitter feed showing conference hashtag tweets directly in the email. Pioneering social media integration within email.

title: Uber - Rate Decrease with Data Visualization
description: Comparative graphs and data visualization showing rate decreases. Bright blue color scheme with minimal text. Charts serve as effective way to illustrate pricing information, making complex data instantly digestible.

title: GrubHub - Interactive Quiz with Saturated GIF Animation
description: Interactive quiz content with saturated GIF animation and storytelling approach. Motion-based graphics capture attention in a crowded inbox. Combines entertainment with data collection.

title: Casper - Sleep Symposium Animated Eye Event Invitation
description: Animated eye GIF is simple but brings the entire design to life. Event invitation for 'Sleep Symposium' with speaker list and a 'more to be announced' hook. Proves that one small animation can transform an otherwise static email.

title: Behance - U99 Conference Speaker Announcement
description: Colorful graphics and textures creating cohesive, stylish aesthetic. Prominent speaker photos with plenty of white space. Targets New York creatives with design that matches the caliber of the audience.

title: Hyggekrog - Warm Welcome with 90% Open Rate
description: Personal brand introduction with warm tone. Beautiful purple background with clean layout and featured performance image. Clear subscriber expectations, loyalty program promotion, and discount coupon. Founder personally writes all emails.

title: Fridja - New Product Launch Selling 25% of Stock Pre-Release
description: High-quality product imagery with emphasized limited-time discount and clear bold CTA. Simple design that lets the product speak. Sold 25% of initial stock before the official release day.
conversion_data: 25% of initial stock sold before release day

title: Because of Marketing - Elegant B&W Event Invitation
description: Black-and-white elegant design with straightforward messaging. Speaker introductions with photos. Drove 40% of total ticket sales directly from email alone.

title: ProShot - Save Corals Save 20% Cause-Driven Campaign
description: Stunning coral imagery connecting environmental cause to discount. 'Save Corals Save 20%' ties purpose to purchase. Urgency messaging paired with breathtaking underwater photography.

title: Kent Brushes - 839% Revenue Increase Email Campaign
description: Email marketing drove 839% quarterly increase in email-generated revenue. Black Friday campaign was particularly successful. Email became the highest converting channel, driving just under 10% of total revenue from July-November.
conversion_data: 839% quarterly increase in email revenue; highest converting channel driving ~10% of total revenue

title: Draper James - AI-Optimized Copy Driving 10x First Purchases
description: Used real-time AI to improve email campaign copy, creating messages that compelled subscribers to become first-time buyers. First-time purchases increased by 10x and repeat purchases boosted by 30%.
conversion_data: 10x increase in first-time purchases; 30% boost in repeat purchases

title: DAAG - $139K Revenue Storytelling Newsletter
description: Weekly newsletters using value-first storytelling focused on inspiration rather than constant sales pushes. Generated $139K from newsletters over 11 months. Single email generated $19.2K in just 2 days. Welcome flow added $29.5K.
conversion_data: $139K from newsletters (11 months); $19.2K from single email (2 days); $29.5K from welcome flow; $25.2K recovered via abandoned cart

title: LandCafe.pl - 54% Sales Rate Educational Welcome Series
description: Educational welcome series following Learn, Like, Trust, Buy framework. Multi-email sequence educated subscribers about artisanal coffee before purchase. Achieved 41.30% sales rate during welcome series and 54% total sales rate.
conversion_data: 41.30% sales rate during welcome series; 54% total sales rate; 13.30% discount email sales rate

title: Med&Beauty - $43K from 10 Newsletters with 873% ROI
description: Generated $43K revenue from just 10 newsletters. Short, visually engaging product-specific emails leveraging social media engagement to drive email conversions. 873% ROI in 5 months with 1,700+ welcome flows completed.
conversion_data: $43K from 10 newsletters; 873% ROI in 5 months; 1,700+ welcome flows; 1,400+ carts recovered

title: Eveline Cosmetics - AI Product Recommendations Adding $7K/Month
description: Combined segmentation with AI-powered cross-selling to increase average order value. Single campaign generated $13K. AI product recommendations added $7K to carts in one month. 7x ROI on email marketing.
conversion_data: $13K from single campaign; $7K added to carts via AI recommendations (one month); 7x ROI

title: Liquor Loot - Witty Cart Abandonment Subject Line
description: When a shopper leaves behind a subscription box, they receive a message with the subject line 'Your cart is sobering up.' Witty and unusual subject line improves open rate and conversion rate. Perfect brand voice alignment.

title: Girlfriend Collective - Sustainable Cart Recovery
description: Minimalistic email template with clever copy: 'Looks like you left something cute and sustainable in your cart. Don't worry, we saved it for you.' Ties brand values (sustainability) directly into the recovery message.

title: Ugmonk - Owner Personal Outreach Cart Recovery
description: Hyper-personalization with direct message from the owner/designer. Uses first name, asks for feedback, offers multiple action paths including reply option. Feels like a genuine personal email, not marketing automation.

title: Whisky Loot - Objection-Resolving Cart Email with Emoji Bullets
description: Conversation-focused rather than conversion-focused. Addresses objections directly with 'Still thinking about it?' heading. Uses emoji bullet points to list benefits. Resolves concerns instead of pushing for the sale.

title: Dollar Shave Club - Social Proof Cart Recovery with Bear Image
description: Laid-back, everyday language combined with intriguing visuals (bear covering its eyes). Features relevant customer testimonials as social proof. The unexpected bear imagery stops the scroll and the testimonials close the sale.

title: Moschino - Invitation-Style Luxury Cart Recovery
description: Invitation-style design with gradient shadowing and careful logo/product placement. Extends brand design language into the recovery sequence. Proves luxury brands can do cart abandonment without cheapening their image.

subject_line: Headmaster Kel'thuzad has chosen you as his pupil, Marilia
title: Blizzard - Immersive In-Character Storytelling Email
description: Subscriber is personally chosen through in-game character narrative. Personalization becomes a story element rather than a marketing tactic. The recipient isn't reading marketing; they're receiving a message from a game character.

subject_line: You've earned a new badge. Check it out!
title: Grammarly - Weekly Usage Stats with Achievement Badges
description: Gaming vibe with achievement badges based on writing stats. Weekly usage update doubles as engagement driver. Social media share buttons encourage viral spread. Turns mundane usage data into a reward system.

subject_line: Need a new look? Just add foil
title: MOO - Gold Foil Upsell with Bold Messaging
description: Elegant design with 'Be bold. Be gold' messaging. High-quality product visuals with red color psychology for conversion. The visual treatment mirrors the premium foil product being sold. Design reinforces the value proposition.

subject_line: New ways to earn free drinks and food
title: Starbucks - Loyalty Program Join Campaign
description: Solid branding elements with trademark green throughout. App download CTA with reward emphasis. Clean, recognizable design that reinforces the Starbucks experience. Loyalty program enrollment made effortless.

title: Death Wish Coffee - Inverted Pyramid Loyalty Reward
description: Inverted pyramid model with bold letters. Red CTA button creates strong visual hierarchy. Clear, direct messaging with no distractions. The bold, dark aesthetic perfectly matches the brand's 'world's strongest coffee' positioning.

title: Siete - Family Story Welcome Email with 'Juntos es Mejor'
description: Uses welcome email to share the heartfelt family story behind the brand. Emphasizes inclusivity and dietary consciousness with the core message 'Juntos es Mejor' (Together is Better). Emotional connection before product pitch.
conversion_data: Welcome emails generate 320% more revenue vs other promotional emails

title: Duolingo - Short & Sweet Mascot-Driven Welcome
description: Short and sweet welcome email introducing mascot Duo the owl. Focuses on one key action: building a 7-day streak as the most likely path to habit formation. One message, one CTA, maximum clarity.

subject_line: There's nothing as good as knowing
title: Ritual - Empathy-Led Welcome Addressing Customer Reservations
description: Addresses customer reservations with empathy in the welcome sequence. Includes educational resources to build trust before asking for a purchase. Acknowledges that supplements require trust, then methodically builds it.
conversion_data: 45% of first-time purchases by new subscribers occur within 24 hours of opting in

subject_line: Scotch news is the best kind of news. Welcome!
title: The Glenlivet - Interactive Quiz Welcome for Audience Segmentation
description: Welcome email with interactive quiz for audience segmentation. Collects preference data while entertaining new subscribers. Turns data collection into an engaging experience rather than a form fill.

title: Webflow - Account Verification with Value-Driving Video Tutorial
description: Account verification email that goes beyond basic confirmation. Includes authentication link backup and value-driving video tutorial. Turns a mandatory transactional moment into an onboarding opportunity.

title: Haoma - Order Confirmation with Tree-Planting Brand Mission
description: Order confirmation that weaves brand messaging about tree-planting into transaction details. Recycling note reinforces sustainability values. Every touchpoint, even transactional, reinforces the brand story.

title: Ten Thousand - Shipping Confirmation with UGC Social Content
description: Shipping confirmation with motivational copy 'The wait is over, now go get after it.' Prominent tracking CTA plus user-generated social content. Transforms a shipping notification into brand engagement.

title: Hulu - Cancellation Confirmation with Win-Back CTA
description: Cancellation confirmation that doubles as a win-back attempt. Clear, concise copy in just two lines. Reactivation-focused design. Proves even goodbye emails can drive re-acquisition.

title: Flourish/Nissan - DMA Gold Award Winner for Email
description: Won Gold in Email category and Bronze in Automotive and Launch categories at 2025 DMA Awards. Partnership between agency Flourish and Nissan. Recognized for excellence in strategy, creativity, and results.

title: Compare the Market - 5 DMA Awards Including Grand Prix Runner-Up
description: Won 5 DMA Awards: Gold in Marketing Automation, Customer Acquisition, AI, and Financial Services, plus Silver in Data & Insight. Grand Prix runner-up. The most awarded email campaign of 2025.
conversion_data: 5 DMA Awards including 4 Golds; Grand Prix runner-up

title: McDonald's UK - 4 DMA Awards for Always-On Lifecycle Program
description: Won 4 DMA Awards for outstanding Always-On lifecycle program including Monday Offers (weekly deals for mobile orders) and MyMcDonald's Rewards (points on every order). Recognized for sustained email excellence across the full customer journey.

title: Tracksmith - CEO Letter Narrative-Driven Newsletter
description: Narrative-driven letter format from the CEO emphasizing storytelling over sales messaging. The newsletter reads like a personal letter from a fellow runner, not a brand. Builds deep emotional connection through authentic voice.

title: Unsplash - User-Generated Photography Newsletter
description: User-generated imagery as the primary content with minimal text. Images serve as the design hero. The newsletter is essentially a curated photography exhibition delivered to your inbox.

title: Penguin Random House - Preference-Personalized Book Newsletter
description: Personalized content based on reader genre preferences via preference center. Segmented sends ensure each subscriber gets relevant book recommendations. Personalized emails get 6x the transaction rates.

title: Asana - Clean Geometric Modernist Newsletter
description: Three-section layout with trademark clean, geometric, almost modernist illustrations. Seasonal theming with consistent branding. Each section clicks through to an ebook or relevant content piece. Design that is unmistakably Asana.

title: Biovie - Auto-Resend Campaign with 45% Open Rate Boost
description: Uses auto-resend feature to reach subscribers who didn't open the first send. Simple technique that increased open rates by 45%. Proves that sometimes the best design improvement is just getting seen.

title: Warby Parker - Behind-the-Scenes Origin Story Email
description: Shares founding struggles and vulnerabilities in welcome sequence. Behind-the-scenes origin story showing how the company came to disrupt eyewear. Transparency about the journey builds trust and emotional investment.

title: Morning Brew - Day-in-the-Life Product Integration Newsletter
description: Invites subscribers into daily routines showing authentic product/content use without overt sales pitches. The newsletter format itself becomes the product. Lifestyle integration over promotional messaging.

title: Ganni - Fashion Welcome with Discount and Inclusivity
description: Clean layout featuring a few choice fashion picks with discount offer. Brief welcome message, discount code, shop CTA, and inclusivity messaging. The welcome email as brand manifesto.

title: Venmo - QR Code Re-engagement Email
description: QR code at the start immediately captures attention. Light on both graphics and text. The QR code bridges email to mobile app in a single scan. Re-engagement through technology rather than guilt.

title: Vimeo - Droste Effect Interactive Quiz Email
description: Droste effect (a picture appearing within itself) creates visual intrigue. Interactive quiz collects subscriber data while entertaining. The recursive visual design is a conversation starter that drives engagement.

title: Methodical Coffee - Personalized Seasonal Recommendations
description: Personalized coffee recommendations for Christmas season. Cobalt gray and tan palette creates sophisticated aesthetic. Design that feels curated specifically for each subscriber's taste profile.

title: Rael - Cheeky Brand-Personality Cart Recovery
description: Creative and cheeky tone to convey brand personality in cart recovery. Humorous approach makes abandoned cart emails feel like a friend reminding you, not a brand nagging you.

title: Forever 21 - Price Drop Notification for Abandoned Items
description: Personalization by notifying subscribers of price drops on previously viewed items. White space effectively addresses the challenge of showing products without clutter. Turns abandoned cart into a price alert service.

title: Clint Orms - Celebrity Partnership with Music Sampling in Email
description: Features country artist Lyle Lovett as VIP customer with music sampling embedded in email. Hybrid approach for local and remote audiences. Adding music enables remote participation while locals are invited to a concert.

title: Maze - DISCO CONF Countdown Clock Registration
description: Countdown clock that quickly boosts engagement by creating urgency for a design conference. 'Register for free' CTA leverages the appeal of free events. Countdown timers in emails are proven engagement drivers.

subject_line: I'm sorry it didn't work out
title: BetaList - Breakup Email with Alternative Subscription
description: Unsubscribe/break-up email that offers alternative (weekly newsletter) to re-engage before fully losing the subscriber. 'I'm sorry it didn't work out' subject line treats the relationship with humor and humanity.

title: Paul Mitchell - Humorous Unsubscribe Retention Email
description: Humor-based approach to convince subscribers not to unsubscribe. Charming tone attempts to reverse the decision with personality rather than desperation. Retention through entertainment.

title: Ami Ami - 90s Cultural References Wine Brand Email
description: Colorful graphics aligned perfectly with the popular upmarket wine-in-cartons brand. 90s cultural references target millennials and Gen Z. The design breaks wine industry conventions with playful, irreverent energy.

title: Netflix - In-Show Feedback Collection Email
description: Three different CTAs all driving the same goal of collecting show feedback. Like/dislike rating system redirects to homepage. Turns passive viewing into active engagement with minimal friction.

title: The Venetian Resort - Birthday Email with Personalized Landing Page
description: Sent one month before birthday with dedicated personalized landing page. 25% discount creates compelling birthday offer. The early timing ensures booking before the actual birthday arrives.

title: Boxycharm - Animated Arrow GIF Scroll-Driver
description: Uses animated down-arrow GIFs as visual cues to maintain engagement and direct attention. Teases surprise offer to encourage scrolling. Multiple animated arrows create a guided journey through the email content.

title: Rifle Paper Co. - Non-Pushy Sale Follow-Up
description: Non-pushy follow-up approach finding valid excuse to re-engage without urgency language. Announces new sale items without scarcity pressure. Recommends product categories gently. Proves sales emails don't need aggressive tactics.

title: Snif - Sold-Out Social Proof Product Launch
description: Emphasizes launch success through 'sold out' messaging as ultimate social proof. Shoppers need no more proof than SOLD OUT status. Pre-order prompt follows after urgency is established. Scarcity as the entire design concept.

title: Magic Spoon - Layered Social Proof with Contrasting Colors
description: Drives trust through layered social proof with review snippets plus audience story snapshots. Creates anticipation with engaging copy. Uses contrasting colors for maximum visual impact. Multiple forms of proof reinforce each other.

title: Moment - Device-Segmented Product Launch Email
description: Segments like a boss - the iPhone 13 buyer instantly feels invited. Targets specific device owners with relevant accessories. Outlines features clearly without requiring click-through. Segmentation makes the product feel personally relevant.
